items.text:
Hi Marcus,

A package arrived for you today at the Brooks Hall mailroom. Please pick it up from Ms. Reyes by this Friday, May 8, before 6:00 PM. Remember to bring your student ID. A $3.50 holding fee applies after that date.

Thanks,
Dana, Resident Assistant

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Marcus bring to the mailroom?
questions.explanation: The email instructs Marcus to bring his student ID when collecting the package.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about collecting the package after May 8?

options.A: It will be returned to the sender
options.B: It must be signed for twice
options.C: It will involve an extra charge
options.D: It can only be done by mail

questions.explanation: Since a $3.50 holding fee applies after May 8, picking up later implies paying an extra charge.

items.text: NOTICE: Heating maintenance will be performed in Anderson Hall on March 3 and March 4. Temperatures in classrooms may drop noticeably during these days. Occupants are advised to dress warmly. Questions may be directed to the Facilities Office in Room 110.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what may happen during the maintenance?
questions.explanation: The notice warns that temperatures in classrooms may drop noticeably, meaning the rooms may become cold.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about people using Anderson Hall on March 3?

options.A: They should plan for chilly conditions
options.C: They should expect the building locked
options.D: They should arrive earlier than usual

questions.explanation: Because occupants are advised to dress warmly during the temperature drop, they should plan for chilly conditions.

items.text: NOTICE: Campus Wi-Fi will be unavailable on Tuesday, April 12, from 1:00 AM to 4:00 AM while network servers are upgraded. The outage will affect all residence halls and the library. Service is expected to resume by 4:00 AM. Contact the IT Help Desk with concerns.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long is the Wi-Fi outage scheduled to last?
questions.explanation: The outage runs from 1:00 AM to 4:00 AM, a span of about three hours.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students living in residence halls on April 12?

options.A: They will lose access to the library entirely
options.B: They may be unable to go online overnight
options.C: They must reset their network passwords
options.D: They should report to the IT Help Desk

questions.explanation: Since the outage affects residence halls during the early morning hours, residents may be unable to go online overnight.

items.text: NOTICE: Air-conditioning repairs will take place in Whitman Hall on June 18. Rooms may feel unusually warm throughout the day. A portable fan may be borrowed from the front desk for a refundable $5 deposit. Direct any questions to Building Services in Room 220.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how can occupants obtain a portable fan?

options.A: By reserving one online in advance
options.B: By requesting one from Building Services
options.C: By leaving a refundable deposit at the desk
options.D: By bringing one from another building

questions.explanation: A fan may be borrowed from the front desk in exchange for a refundable $5 deposit.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about conditions in Whitman Hall on June 18?

options.B: Rooms may be closed to occupants
options.C: Rooms may be moved to another floor
options.D: Rooms may be cleaned during repairs

questions.explanation: Because the air-conditioning is being repaired and rooms may feel unusually warm, conditions may be uncomfortably hot.
